frameworks.name: active vision / fixational eye movements
frameworks.one_line: Gaze shifts during fixation actively shape retinal input for acuity.

core_question: Do fixational eye movements contribute to human visual acuity by tailoring retinal input to the stimulus?
key_assumptions: Eyes are never still even during attempted fixation
Luminance flow on the retina carries usable discriminative information
manipulations: High-acuity task requiring fine spatial discrimination
Varying spatial layout of the stimulus
key_findings: Humans precisely shift gaze to compensate for stimulus spatial layout
Resulting retinal luminance modulations carry discriminative information at scales finer than the stimulus
Subjects with better control of fixational eye movements achieve higher acuity
interpretations: Fixational eye movements are a critical component of human visual acuity
Acuity emerges from a coupling of oculomotor control with stimulus structure, not optics alone
